1.label: generic
1.description: Most generic patterns (broad transformations with minimal context). Example: chlorination.

2.label: functional_group_specific
2.description: More specific patterns that define a functional group / substrate context. Example: chlorination involving an acid chloride (or other defined functional group context).

3.label: reagent_specific
3.description: Most specific patterns that explicitly encode a reagent driving the reaction. Example: chlorination with POCl5 / POCl3 / SOCl2.

meaning.5: Highest within-class preference (use when you strongly believe this template should win tie-breaks).
meaning.1: Lowest preference (use for weak/ambiguous templates, e.g., solvents/salts acting like reagents).
